import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.stats import norm

def task_func(df, column, bins=30, density=True, alpha=0.6, color="g", seed=None):
    if seed is not None:
        np.random.seed(seed)

    df[column] = df[column].astype('float')

    data = df[column].values
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

    # Plot histogram
    n, bins_hist = np.histogram(data, bins=bins)
    bin_edges = np.append(bins_hist[:-1], data.max())

    if density:
        plt.bar(bin_edges, n / len(data), alpha=alpha, color=color)
    else:
        plt.bar(bin_edges, n, alpha=alpha, color=color)
    plt.xlabel(column)
    plt.ylabel('Frequency')

    # Fit normal distribution and plot curve
    mu, std = norm.fit(data, floc=0)
    x = np.linspace(bin_edges[0], bin_edges[-1], 100)
    y = norm.pdf(x, mu, std)
    plt.plot(x, y, 'r--', linewidth=2, alpha=alpha)

    # Add information about the mean and standard deviation of the fitted normal distribution
    plt.title(f'Histogram of {column} with Fitted Normal Distribution (mu: {round(mu, 3)}, std: {round(std, 3)})')
    return plt.gca(), mu, std
